notes.note: A disreputable rumour-monger of 43 BC who shuttled gossip between Octavian's camp and Decimus Brutus; the wry phrase "a man entirely true to himself" marks him as untrustworthy.

Cicero's notorious bon mot about the young Caesar (Latin laudandum, ornandum, tollendum), where tollere puns on "raise up / honour" and "do away with"; the report that Octavian had heard it caused Cicero lasting embarrassment.
A commission of ten (decemviri) charged with assigning land and rewards to the soldiers; the veterans resented that neither Octavian nor Decimus Brutus had been named to it.

notes.anchor: the Sullan lands and the Campanian district
notes.note: Two reserves of public land available for veteran settlement: territory confiscated and redistributed under Sulla's dictatorship (82-79 BC), and the ager Campanus, the rich state land around Capua long contested in Roman politics.

notes.anchor: The ninth day before the Kalends of June, at Eporedia.
notes.note: Dated by inclusive Roman counting back from the Kalends (first) of June, i.e. 24 May 43 BC; Eporedia (modern Ivrea) was a colony of Cisalpine Gaul at the foot of the Alps.
